evidence: US Fed News, Nov 9 2012, republishing verbatim a San Diego County press release dated Nov. 7: 'Those ballots made up about 63 percent of the total vote. The remaining 37 percent of ballots - about 475,000 of them - are vote-by-mail and provisional ballots'. Certified final 1,203,265 minus 475,000 = 728,265 counted (60.52%).

San Diego Union-Tribune Web Edition, Nov 9 2016, 'Issa still leading, barely': 'as of late Wednesday afternoon... Across San Diego County there are 620,000 uncounted provisional and mail-in ballots... San Diego County is not expected to release more results until Thursday.' Certified final 1,346,513 minus 620,000 = 726,513 counted (53.96%).

Fresno Bee, Nov 8 2018, 'It's all over but the counting': 'Election Day is over, but there are still 100,000 vote-by-mail and provisional ballots left to count in Fresno County. Registrar Brandi Orth said Wednesday morning that the process of counting about 76,000 mail-in ballots had begun' (plus 24,000 provisionals) and 'Fresno County will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon.' Certified final 256,972 minus 100,000 = 156,972 counted through election night (61.09%).

Unofficial final results from the county Registrar of Voters show Kevin Jeffries leading Buster ... with all precincts reporting. But about 183,000 vote-by-mail, provisional and damaged ballots remained to be counted. Certified final 669,627 minus 183,000 = 486,627 counted through election night (72.67%).

Riverside County Registrar of Voters Rebecca Spencer said she had a crew of about 300 workers processing ballots overnight Tuesday. According to Wednesday vote totals, they counted about 300,000 ballots. "We're not counting anything today," Spencer said. 300,000/650,545 = 46.12%.

In San Bernardino County, elections workers counted about 322,000 ballots overnight Tuesday. On Wednesday, about 150,816 ballots remained to be counted, along with 8,929 damaged ballots and 43,818 provisional and other ballots that require processing. 322,000/546,041 = 58.97%. CORRECTION (2026-07-09): reclassified REFUTED_AS_PLATEAU / retained as a documented ceiling, comparable=false -- the article's own schedule shows a pending Friday release and the supporting leg was a same-article arithmetic cross-check (disallowed).

About 60,000 mail-in and 45,000 provisional ballots in San Bernardino County were uncounted as of Thursday morning, said Elections Office spokeswoman Felisa Cardona. Ballots cast at polling places and mailed prior to Election Day were all counted by 5:15 a.m. Wednesday. 589,611 minus 105,000 = 484,611 (82.19%), retained as a ceiling, comparable=false.

In San Bernardino County, as of Wednesday at 4 p.m., the Registrar of Voters had counted ballots cast by 218,993 people. An estimated 232,000 mail-in ballots still need to be processed. 218,993/458,946 = 47.72%, retained as a ceiling (this is the county's own canvass-resumption slot, not the 9:40 a.m. election-night posting), comparable=false.
